ocr.text: The extract from Albizia adianthifolia leaves has garnered attention due to its potential bioactive properties, particularly
regarding its phenolic content. The data table provides insight into the concentration of key phytochemicals, specifically the
levels of gallic acid and rutoside, measured in milligrams per gram of lyophilized powder. The extraction process yields an
impressive 30.172 ± 0.863 mg of gallic acid per gram, highlighting the extract's potential as a source of antioxidants. In
addition, the presence of rutoside is quantified at 0.525 ± 0.001 mg per gram, suggesting a noteworthy contribution to the

extract's overall phytochemical profile. 

To ensure the reliability of the data, strict inclusion and exclusion criteria were
implemented during participant selection for associated studies. The effectiveness of the extract's antioxidant activity is further
demonstrated by its scavenging percentage, recorded at 58.189 ± 0.601%. These metrics underscore the significance of Albizia
adianthifolia as a candidate for further research into its therapeutic applications, particularly in the context of natural
antioxidants.
Phenols  Flavonoids  DPPH
(mg gallic acid/g lyophilized powder)  (mg rutoside/g lyophilized powder)  scavenged%
Albizia adianthifolia leaves extract  30.172 ± 0.863  0.525 ± 0.001  58.189 ± 0.601
Inclusion criteria  Exclusion criteria

• Speaks as first language the target language—primary language was defined as a language that the participant grew up speaking and/or reading from his or her childhood, or as determined by sociocultural norms such as work environment of his or her home country  • Declines interest in participating
• Has a severe debilitating illness precluding participation
• Does not speak the target language
• Has lived in Qatar for at least 12 months in the past 3 years
• Provides verbal informed consent
